item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, uh, I remember reading this back when it was new, I actively remember because I was supposed to be focusing on stuff in class. hey i had finished my work

Realized I never said this: It's great, and is an easy +1 from me. You can feel the pain from both Alex and Noah over the course of the article, and it's just… Aaa, it's good. The MTF being disconnected from the humanity of their actions makes the message hit harder in a subtler way, because in their eyes and crosshairs, it's a reality warper and nothing but.

For what it's worth, this article is very frustrating on mobile devices, because they don't do hovertext too well, but I mean, many a skip seems to disagree with mobile, so don't worry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ proofreads
- please let me know if these were tonal choices!
 Olson: Alright. How did do you know SCP-5117-1?

how did do he, indeed 
 I gave you to those text logs to remember

words swapped? 
 Muñoz: If you're so proud of your kills counts,

one of those words probably shouldn't be pluralized.

obviously this is still a +1 article, love what you did with the hovertext here, and honestly i like how you wrote the characters here. it seems like only the MTF grunts don't have an appropriate level of…humanity about them, and that feels like an appropriate thematic choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic narrative, but I would have loved to hear more about the rings themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is written very well. It hit me deeply. I had no problems understanding any of the story. Each element has a good reason to be there. It’s not too short or long. I love SCPs that question the humanity of the Foundation’s actions, especially when it’s done this well.

Enthusiastic +1 with the hope that this receives more attention in the future! :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alex?
Alex?

A pretty emotional scip. Nice.

+1.

Also, one typo: "the severity of you actions" in the final collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Literally just got to this PM for a critique request, and I see now that it has been posted. So…

Critique is Coming Late
But is Hopefully Still Helpful

Line-by-Line / Technical Critique

Access is to be restricted to individuals with explicit authorization from a Site Director Calloway.

Are there multiple Site Director Calloway's? Heheh.

[…] briefly reanimated via SCP-5117s anomaly […]

Possessive, should be "SCP-5117's".

The following file is incomplete. Personnel should review the following files for additional information:

You use "following file(s)" twice to refer to different things. Kind of confusing.

For the record, please identify yourself

Missing a period.

Whether or not he’s gonna keep working at the Foundation […]

G-gonna? That doesn't feel professional at all!

Noting right now, that by this line:

SCP-5117-1: wait Im not the only one??!?

Cora: I didn’t say that

I had the thought "oh I am enjoying this".

As of present, Senior Containment Engineer Alexander Robert Cora has committed at least three (3) infractions of significant concern […]

You proceed to list four. So…?

Big Picture / Conceptual Critique

Heyyy! This is good! The only things I can say are that I think that the RAISA part wouldn't have been handled by RAISA. That would have been put in with the rest of the documents, just not sure of why it was done in this format. But other than that, this is simply good. It's a story about humanity, not new to the Foundation, but done in a very fun and saddening way. Really felt like real people.

Oh, there is one other thing. The format for the "Alex?" bit didn't make much sense to me. The article was good enough for me to take it in stride, but I raised eyebrows. Is there no better way to show that Noah is talking in an inhuman fashion? What is this supposed to represent in the SCP file?

Anyways, that's a +1, take it and have a good day.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: In Addendum 2, there's still a SCP-XXXX-1 near the end, just so you know.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Finally got my second skip ever up and running!

Huge thanks to everyone involved: DrCaroll, for giving me the initial inspiration, salvaged from an old idea, JakdragonX and AbsentmindedNihilist for the first draft critique, cybersqyd for reviewing the second draft and providing a thorough line-by-line, and finally, HammerMaiden for invaluable help finalizing, especially with streamlining and flow. Not to mention all the people I was inspired by, and all the people whose articles I took formatting ideas from.

Additional shoutout to MrWrong for the awesome mouse over formatting, lifted from his awesome Memetics Orientation tale, and djkaktus for the Anomaly Classification Bar and theme.

Thanks, everyone!
